The center of activity In the
Fight for the right of free speech
pm the streets will shift next week
from the courts to the arena Of
political action.


The movement to inaugurate a
decent street speaking ordinance
by the power of the initiative will
be set In motion, and it is hoped by
Ibis means to compel the city au-
(horities to solve a question that
could be settled in is minutes.


This will not be a movement by
he Industrial Workers solely, but
largely by the taxpayers and labor
ing people of the city, who are en-
firely out of sympathy with the
farce being carried on by the police
department.


1t is proposed to secure the sig.
natures of 20 per cent of the voters
pr the city asking the council to
submit to the approval of the peo-
pie the street speaking ordinance
prepared by Attorney Fred II.
Moore. The petitions will be in
fealiness Monday and due an
houncement will be made as to
where they can be found.
